raw_consumer_statement:
On XXXX XXXX, I could not log into my Citibank CostcoAnywhere account to pay my bill. A call to customer service revealed that someone requested a replacement card for my account. On XX/XX/2024, a replacement card was mailed to that person, first name XXXX, living somewhere in Pennsylvania. The address, email, phone number and password on my account where changed at the same time. The same person placed a travel notice on the account and made 38 charges in XXXX using the replacement card from XXXX. I received no communication from Citibank about any of these changes to my account. 
The account was closed, fraud dispute filed, address, phone number and email where updated and a new account was issued. I did receive email notifications of these changes. 
XX/XX/2024 I called Fraud and Disputes department to provide additional information. The representative located the " take over '' within the online Chat records. She confirmed that a replacement card was mailed to XXXX XXXX. I confirmed that I do not know this person and did not authorize anyone to use my credit card. Also confirmed I had made no charges in XXXX and have never been there. The original card was and is still in my possession. 
XX/XX/2024 I received a letter stating dispute was closed. " The card 's XXXX chip was used to make the pisputed transactions at a chip enabled card reader. You verified that your card was in your possession at the time of the transactions, so we do not consider this fraudulent activity. '' XX/XX/2024 Appeals with lawyer 's letter where mailed to XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX. I recived an email confirmation of receipt of new information, but to date have received no further communication from Citibank other that advertising and payment due. notice
